feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Integration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Cannot import data from Concur
entry.content:
Currently in SAP  we have not been able to import data from Concur

The attached document shows the error received

entry.title: MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.engine.services.jndi.persistent.exceptions720.Namespac in PI
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
We are facing an issue in SAP PI/PO where the message is processed successfully and delivered to the target system, but the sender receives an HTTP 500 error. In the logs we see the following:

MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.engine.services.jndi.persistent.exceptions720.NameNotFoundException: Object not found in lookup of NamespaceMapperBean.

Even though message delivery is successful, this exception still appears and causes the user to see a 500 Internal Server Error.

Could you please advise what could cause this..

Thank you

entry.title: MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.engine.services.jndi.persistent.exceptions720 in PI/PO
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
We are facing an issue in SAP PI/PO where the message is processed successfully and delivered to the target system, but the sender receives an HTTP 500 error. In the logs we see the following:

MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.engine.services.jndi.persistent.exceptions720.NameNotFoundException: Object not found in lookup of NamespaceMapperBean.

Even though message delivery is successful, this exception still appears and causes the user to see a 500 Internal Server Error.

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP Edge Integration Cell Offline Mode
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We are going to leverage Hybrid solution of Integration Suite and I have few open questions.

Could any one from SAP reply on this and share any documentation ?

1) Offline mode for Edge Integration Cell ( where no connectivity between Cloud and On-premises), As per SAP, they don’t provide any support after 48 hours of offline mode.

Is Edge Cell runtime will stop processing after 48 hours ?
Or
There is any option to extent support from SAP side.

2) Can we start/stop or re-send message from Edge Cell directly during offline mode ?
As per SAP documentation, it’s only possible from cloud.

Is there any solution or custom development required to action on those messages or channel during offline mode ?

Thanks,
Bijaya

entry.title: SAP BTP Integration Suite Cloud to SAP S4 Private Cloud
entry.content:
Dear All,

Good Day!

Is there any documentation/steps to integrate SAP BTP Integration Suite Cloud to SAP S4 Private Cloud via Oauth2.0 Bearer Token.

Am not finding any documentation from SAP.

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: Error: While connecting from SAP BTP Integration Suite to SAP S4 Cloud Odata
entry.content:
Hello Team,

 

I'm getting below error while connecting from SAP BTP Integration Suite Cloud to SAP S4 Cloud Odata service.

"com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException: HTTP Request failed with error : <HOST>: No address associated with hostname, cause: java.net.UnknownHostException: <HOST>: No address associated with hostname"

 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: IDoc Reaches SAP PI but No cXML Message Generated
entry.content:
I am working on an SAP PI/PO scenario where an outbound IDoc needs to be converted to cXML and sent to our partner’s Boomi endpoint.

My sender IDoc channel is working fine, and the mapping executes successfully.
But the receiver HTTP channel (CC_RCV_XXXX) stays in red status, and no cXML is being delivered to the endpoint. but in Open Message i can the CXML file. and status is waiting with 400 after that it is successful with 401 i

What I see

  • The Connection Test in the receiver channel shows “Connection OK.”

  • But the channel status in monitoring is red.

  • Runtime messages fail before the cXML gets delivered.

  • The partner endpoint is reachable and responds in Postman.

What I need help with

Why does the communication channel remain red, even when the connection test succeeds?

What are the possible runtime issues (authentication method, URL format, Content-Type, SSL, mapping, HTTP error codes, etc.) that can cause:

  • Ping OK, but

  • Actual message delivery failing, causing the channel to stay red?

If anyone has run into this type of issue, please share what the root cause was or what logs I should focus on.

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP PO: Empty Request Payload (REST to RFC)
entry.content:
Hi,

There is a requirement to send the request based from URL query params and map it to the target field via UDF, this is already working however I don't see any way to send empty payload as it is still looking for the source payload in Dummy Message Type even if it's not mapped to the target. If I add it it's working if not, I'm getting the following error response:

but If I added the source payload of the dummy message type it is working, although it's only dummy - it seems like it still requires it.

Can you anyone share what approach I can do to achieve the empty request payload?

Thank you!

entry.title: RFC using Python in Excel
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I was asking myself if there is any chance to have a RFC to SAP (I'm still on ECC 6) from within Python in Excel.

Any hints is very much appreciated.

entry.title: LMS to EdCast Integration
entry.content:
Hello SF Folks, 

Good day! I hope you are doing well.  

I need help regarding connection of Edcast and LMS integration. 

We were able to establish the connection of Edcast and LMS integration, and we can view completions and assignments. However, when we try to fetch content in Postman, we receive a 200-success response, but no data is returned in production and test environment.

After reviewing both the sandbox and production environments, we confirmed that no content data is being returned from the API when tested through Postman or Workato.

May we know if there are missing configuration in SuccessFactors side or is there any list that we can follow to make sure that EdCast Vendor has properly configured the system on their side? We follow this instruction below:

SuccessFactors Integrations Beginners Guide- Expl... - SAP Community

Anything that I missed? Please refer to the attached file.

Appreciate your response. 

Happy Holidays! 

Thanks, 
Joerom Ramos

entry.title: Problem using InventoryGenExits with products across different bin locations
entry.content:
I am working with InventoryGenExits and it usually works fine. However, I now have an issue with an item that has two bin locations. For example:

  • BinAbsEntry 1 → Warehouse "001", BinCode "001-UBICACIÓN-DE-SISTEMA" (36 units)

  • BinAbsEntry 3 → Warehouse "001", BinCode "001-MANTENIMIENTO" (5 units)

By default, I always need to withdraw products from BinCode "001-UBICACIÓN-DE-SISTEMA". I tried sending the following JSON structure:  

array:4 [  "DocDate" => "20251217"
  "DocDueDate" => "20251217"
  "Comments" => "Entregas insumos pañol 17-12-2025, FORTINWEB, realizado por JUAN MORENO"
  "DocumentLines" => array:1 [
    0 => array:9 [
      "ItemCode" => "SHEP0018"
      "Quantity" => 1.0
      "WarehouseCode" => "001"
      "CostingCode" => "EF0004"
      "U_PG_Retira" => 577
      "UseBaseUnits" => "tYES"
      "UoMEntry" => -1
      "InventoryQuantity" => 1.0
      "BinAllocations" => array:1 [
        0 => array:4 [
          "BinAbsEntry" => 1
          "Quantity" => 1.0
          "BinActionType" => "batFromWarehouse"
          "BaseLineNumber" => 0
        ]
      ]
    ]
  ]]

 

But I get the following error: 1470000341 - The item "SHEP0018" is not fully allocated to the location "001".

How can I force the system to take the quantity only from the specific bin location I define (BinAbsEntry 1) .  Can someone please help me?

entry.title: ODATA_CALLING_ERROR: How to configure Resource Path in OData Sender Adapter
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on an OData to JDBC integration where we are trying to fetch IDoc status information from SAP and update it on the client side.

This is a CDS view with parameters exposed as ODATA which we are trying to consume from ODATA Sender channel.
We have created an HTTP Destination as shown below:

Destination Name: ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS
URL:
https://agvdwdci01.corp.mooginc.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS_CDS?sap-client=250&sap-language=EN

Resource Path : ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS(p_days=7)/Set
OData Sender Adapter and OData structure snapshots are attached for reference.

However, we are encountering the following error in the PO channel:

ODATA_CALLING_ERROR: OData call failed
404 : NOT_FOUND [ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS(p_days=7)/Set]

The same destination works correctly when tested directly in the browser as well as via Postman.

Could someone please advise what the correct Resource Path should be in the OData channel configuration to make this work successfully?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Custom Portlet Notifications via CPI to Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
Detailed Problem Description / Question

I’m looking for guidance (or an SAP KBA reference) around sending notifications from SAP SuccessFactors to Microsoft Teams via SAP CPI, specifically when the source is a custom portlet.

In our scenario, we have a custom portlet in SuccessFactors that displays an alert or status message on the SuccessFactors home page. Unlike standard SuccessFactors Teams notifications, this custom portlet does not automatically trigger a Teams notification.

The requirement is as follows:

  • A notification or alert occurs in a custom SuccessFactors portlet

  • This event should be picked up by SAP CPI

  • CPI should then send a notification to a Microsoft Teams channel (webhook)

At this stage, we understand that:

  • SuccessFactors does not natively generate MS Teams notifications for custom portlets

  • Any notification would likely require custom integration logic (e.g. OData, Integration Center extract, or custom API trigger)

  • CPI would be responsible for transforming the message and forwarding it to Microsoft Teams

My questions are:

  1. Is there an official SAP KBA or documentation that confirms custom portlets do not support native notifications?

  2. Are there recommended or supported approaches for enabling CPI to detect an event or change from a custom portlet?

  3. Has anyone successfully implemented a CPI → Microsoft Teams notification for a custom SuccessFactors object or portlet, and if so, what approach was used?

Best Regards

Adele Beets

entry.title: Sending JWE/JWS Encrypted JSON Payload from SAP PI/PO to Bank API
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a new requirement from one of our banking partners where payment data needs to be sent from SAP PI/PO to the Bank’s API in encrypted and signed JSON format using JWE/JWS standards.

Integration Scenario:

Source: SAP ECC (IDoc)
Existing Flow:  IDoc → SAP PI/PO → SOAP Call
Expected Flow: IDoc → SAP PI/PO → Encrypted JSON → Bank Corporate API

Security Requirements (as provided by the Bank):

Encryption Mechanism: JWE
Key Encryption Algorithm: RSA-OAEP-256
Content Encryption Method: A256GCM
Signing Algorithm: RS256 (JWS)

Request for Feasibility

I would like to understand whether SAP PI/PO (7.5 single stack) can support JWE + JWS encryption/signing using:

Standard REST Adapter capabilities
Custom Adapter Module
Custom java mapping (UDF)
Or any SAP-recommended approach

If custom development is required, please advise:

Whether external libraries (e.g., Nimbus JOSE + JWT) can be deployed on PI/PO: If yes, then where can i get required JAR files.
Any limitations or performance considerations
Recommended best practices for implementing secure REST communication in PI/PO

Any insights, implementation steps, or past experience handling similar requirements would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: SAP PO - BPM error
entry.content:
Price Tolerance is completed but has not reflected in Finance, even though it was already approved. When we checked in the Fiori system, we observed that the Price Tolerance approval for PO is still pending. It was queried multiple times and all previous queries were approved, but the last approval was not completed successfully.

Upon investigation, we found that the BPM task was suspended and in error. When we checked the reason for the error, we found the following comments:

------------------------
"Process 'ApprovalRefProcess' suspended Process 'PriceTolerance (0)' set to In Error Mapping failed Process 'PriceTolerance (0)' suspended Process 'PriceTolerance (0)' set to In Error Mapping failed Process 'ApprovalCO' suspended Mapping failed java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Value 'KZN Durban Storage' 9 boxes Xtraspace, 99 Chris Hani Road, Briadene, Durban, 4051 Lionel Thangamuthu 060 588 5815 (CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Sep 23 2025 15:12:35)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Oct 28 2025 06:59:55)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Wed Oct 29 2025 11:18:07)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Oct 31 2025 07:49:25)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Nov 04 2025 08:03:29)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Thu Nov 06 2025 13:11:28)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 07 2025 11:53:11)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 14 2025 11:23:08)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Mon Nov 17 2025 08:39:31)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Nov 18 2025 08:46:46)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 21 2025 10:12:19)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Mon Nov 24 2025 10:16:41)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Thu Nov 27 2025 08:43:33)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 28 2025 11:50:02)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Mon Dec 01 2025 07:26:45) of type http://www.sap.com/caf/oup.com/fpa.bl.serreq.modeled#LongText fails validation check: MaxLength=1100 com.sap.mapping.lib.execution.api.exception.TExecutionException: Cannot process assignment RelayTransformer, VoidExpander( TerminalPart.assignment ) at ReqInternalText( TerminalPart.assignment ) at Header( BroadPart.child ) at updateRequest( NarrowPart.child ) at com.sap.caf.oup.com/fpa.bl.serreq.oup/com/caf/app/serr/serreqservice:updateHeaderItemDataV1( BroadPart.child ) at ( NarrowMapping ) For more details, decrease severity of log controller as described in SAP Note 1755360. ---------------------------------------------------------------------

We identified that the failure occurred due to the maximum length validation error. To resolve this, we edited the PO 'header note' in ME22N in SAP and tried to resume the BPM task, but it failed again for the same reason. We then asked the user to resubmit the Price Tolerance for approval, but in the Fiori page, the option to submit for approval was disabled. To address this, we canceled the BPM task for the PO.

Requirement - User need to get this Price Tolerance approved and it should be reflected in Finance.

entry.title: How To Handle JSON Array without an Array Name in SAP PI REST Adapter (Receiver)
entry.content:
Dear Sir,

I have an issue in my current SAP PI project. The Scenario is SOAP to REST.

It seems that I cannot find a way to handle a JSON Array result that has no name, so that I can convert it into XML using REST Adapter receiver.

The JSON result recorded in the monitoring log was.

[
  {
    "A": "Hello",
    "B": 49,
    "C": 39
  }
]

 But what I got was an empty XML.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<ns0:MT_TEST_IN xmlns:ns0="urn:TEST">
</ns0:MT_TEST_IN>

 Here's the REST Adapter (receiver) Configurations for my response message.

And here's my Message Mapping, which is quite straight forward.

I could only find references on how to convert XML to JSON by setting the XML/JSON conversion rules.

Please advice.

Thanks

entry.title: How can an inbound REST GET/POST request from a backend system be conditionally routed in SAP PI/PO
entry.content:
How can an inbound REST GET/POST request from a backend system be conditionally routed in SAP PI/PO based on the presence of an HTTP header parameter (ucasurl), such that:

  • If the HTTP header parameter ucasurl is present, the message is routed to a receiver REST channel that:

    • Uses a dynamic target URL derived from the value of ucasurl, and

    • Executes an HTTP GET call; and

  • If the HTTP header parameter ucasurl is not present, the message is routed to an alternative receiver REST channel that:

    • Uses a static, preconfigured target URL, and

    • Executes an HTTP POST call?

entry.title: Elimination of SAP PI PO systems
entry.content:
Hello. I'm creating this post so we can all share our opinions constructively.

I recently learned that SAP plans to eliminate SAP PI/PO systems to focus solely on Integration Suite.

As someone who works extensively with PO, I feel saddened and angry with SAP. After all these years of
working and continuously acquiring knowledge in PO, SAP seems to have decided to kick it to the curb.
All that knowledge feels like throwing it away. Similarly, SAP is forcing clients who have already invested
money in implementing PO to migrate to Integration Suite. I believe many clients don't want the cloud and prefer
to keep their servers on-premises for security or other reasons. However, SAP doesn't care and is forcing all
clients to move their infrastructure to the cloud... and of course, spend money again.
Personally, I find this despicable.

And although I work with PO, I also work with Integration Suite. I find IS awful, much more difficult,
since you have to develop the business logic by creating huge and complicated iFlows, while in PO,
integrations are generally point-to-point, making them easier to implement and understand.
And then there are options like the Monitor, which is rubbish compared to the PO monitor, where it shows
you all the messages, their log traces, payloads... without you having to do anything, by default.
In IS, if you want to see anything in the monitor, you have to develop it yourself to save headers,
log files, payloads, etc., wasting your time.

Anyway, I find it incredible that SAP has played this game with its customers, forcing them to switch.
And of course, it's forcing us, the employees, to learn another way of doing integrations, throwing away
our knowledge. (I just hope SAP doesn't do very well.)

What do you think?
What's your opinion?
Are you for or against it?

Let's see if we can start a thread to discuss this and get it to someone at SAP.

Thanks. Regards.

entry.title: Transfer objects created in Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO. (Message-type and data-type)
entry.content:
Hi everyone !

    I'm working on a project migrating SAP PI/PO to Cloud Integration NEO (the migration from NEO to Cloud Foundry will be next year). To perform the migration using the migration tool, following the instructions provided by SAP, we created a BTP Trial account and imported all the objects from PI/PO. After the migration, I'm transferring the objects and iFlows to BTP NEO (Cloud Integration). However, Cloud Integration NEO doesn't have the option to import Data-Types or Message-Types that were created during the migration in the Integration Suite of the Trial account. My question is: has anyone done this transfer from Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO before? How do I transfer the data-types and message-types to NEO? And if I have to create them manually in NEO, how do I create these objects in their iFlows on Cloud Integration NEO ?

entry.title: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - SAP S4 to Biztalk 2016
entry.content:
Hello Team,

While Connecting from SAP S4 Cloud to MS Biztalk 2016 it is failing with below error. Totally stucked up with this error for few weeks. SAP states to uplift SAP NCO connector to 3.1.6 it doesn't really help.

 

Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - Stucked in SM58

Latest Update from SAP- Really issue is strange and fishy!!!

Logs:

 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS
